businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and enterprise AI data analytics platform leader, operating on three pillars: Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise clients. FY2025 revenue was $4.48B (+56%). US commercial revenue surpassed $500M. The Pentagon adopted Maven AI as an official program of record, extending it across all US military branches. FY2026 revenue guidance of $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%) shows accelerating growth.
howTheyMakeMoney: Enterprise AI platform (Foundry) - Large enterprise data integration
AIP (AI Platform) - Generative AI integration platform
AIP Bootcamps - Short-term adoption programs for customer conversion

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: ~10x S&P 500 average (~23-25x), ~6x software sector average (~35-40x). Extreme premium, but Forward P/E of 113x based on FY2026E EPS of $1.34 shows rapid compression

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026E EPS of $1.34. Still expensive, but PEG ratio of 2.48 given 65% revenue growth is within high-growth stock range

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 revenue $4.48B. AI demand explosion drove 56% growth. FY2026 guidance of 65% shows acceleration

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Top-tier profitability among software companies. Combined with 82% gross margin, demonstrates powerful earnings capacity

keyMetrics.name: FCF Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Free cash flow of $2.10B. Exceptional cash generation with minimal debt ($229M)

keyMetrics.name: Cash Holdings
keyMetrics.interpretation: Net cash of $6.95B ($2.90/share). Near-zero leverage balance sheet

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Backlog
keyMetrics.interpretation: Multi-year revenue visibility secured. ~2.5x current annual revenue

growthDrivers.title: Maven AI Officially Adopted Across US Military
growthDrivers.description: Deputy Secretary of Defense letter on March 9 designated Maven Smart System as official program of record. Long-term deployment across all military branches secures stable defense revenue

growthDrivers.title: Combat-Proven AI in Iran Conflict
growthDrivers.description: NYT reported Palantir operates a system analyzing Pentagon intelligence using Anthropic AI technology. Real-world combat validation is a powerful driver for additional contracts

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 Revenue Guidance $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%)
growthDrivers.description: Management confident in accelerating growth. US commercial segment emerging as key growth engine, reducing government dependency

growthDrivers.title: Navy ShipOS $448M + Army $10B Contracts
growthDrivers.description: Large military contracts secured in succession. $11.2B revenue backlog provides long-term visibility

growthDrivers.title: Morgan Stanley Raises Target to $205, Hold with Increased Optimism
growthDrivers.description: Analyst Sanjit Singh set $205 target. Not an upgrade but clearly more bullish. UBS $200 (Strong Buy), Wedbush $230 maintained

growthDrivers.title: AIP Bootcamp Accelerating Commercial Conversion
growthDrivers.description: Short-term trial programs rapidly acquiring enterprise clients. US commercial revenue surpassed $500M

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government defense AI track record, top-level security clearance, Maven military adoption, NVIDIA sovereign AI partnership. Combat deployment in Iran provides unmatched validation

moats.description: Ontology platform deeply integrated into government/military systems. Replacement costs are prohibitively high
Top-level security clearances and 20+ years of government trust. Virtually impossible for new entrants to replicate
AIP Bootcamps enable rapid customer acquisition. Ontology data model ecosystem continues to expand
Used for real-time target identification in the Iran conflict. Combat data is the ultimate competitive advantage

competitors.name: Anduril Industries
competitors.detail: Defense AI/drone startup. Hardware-focused, more complementary than competitive with Palantir's software platform

competitors.name: Snowflake / Databricks
competitors.detail: Enterprise data platform competition. Significantly behind in government/defense access

competitors.name: Microsoft / AWS
competitors.detail: Big cloud government AI push. Scale advantage but lacks Ontology-level deep integration

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform. Trails significantly in both revenue scale and growth rate

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: 'Valuation Still Looks Absurd. Here's Why Investors Keep Buying'
recentNews.significance: P/E 239x looks expensive, but some investors view it as 'cheap' given 65% growth and monopolistic defense AI position

recentNews.headline: Wall Street Bear Warns of 55% Drop, Maintains $70 Target
recentNews.significance: An Underperform-rated analyst maintains $70 target, warning of 55% downside. Extreme valuation polarization among analysts

recentNews.headline: Morgan Stanley Sets $205 Target, Strengthens Bullish Stance While Keeping Hold
recentNews.significance: Analyst Sanjit Singh maintains Hold but is clearly more optimistic. Cites revenue acceleration and Maven adoption

recentNews.headline: Pentagon Adopts Maven AI as Core US Military System
recentNews.significance: Deputy Secretary of Defense designated Maven as program of record. Long-term military-wide deployment confirmed

recentNews.headline: Silicon Valley Defense AI Boom, Palantir Deployed in Iran Conflict
recentNews.significance: NYT reports Anthropic AI + Palantir system analyzing Pentagon intelligence in active combat. Real-world AI validation

analystOpinions.notableComment: Morgan Stanley $205 Hold (increasingly bullish), UBS $200 Strong Buy, Wedbush $230. One analyst maintains $70 Underperform warning of 55% downside. Extreme valuation polarization

risks.title: Extreme Valuation, 55% Downside Warning
risks.description: TTM P/E 239x is 10x the S&P 500 average. One analyst warns of 55% downside to $70. Any earnings miss could trigger a sharp selloff

risks.title: War-Dependent Revenue Risk
risks.description: Defense revenue surged with Iran conflict deployment, but conflict resolution could weaken growth momentum. War-based growth raises sustainability concerns

risks.title: Share Dilution
risks.description: Share count increasing 4.67% YoY. Stock-based compensation continuously dilutes existing shareholder value

risks.title: Government Budget Volatility
risks.description: Government shutdowns or defense budget cuts would directly impact revenue. Government revenue share remains significant

risks.title: Ethics/Reputation Risk
risks.description: Growing ethical criticism of AI weapons targeting systems. Swiss magazine lawsuit and press freedom concerns

risks.title: Competition
risks.description: Microsoft, AWS pushing into government AI. Limited near-term threat but worth monitoring long-term

buyReasons.title: Dominant Force in Defense AI
buyReasons.rationale: Maven adopted as official US military system. 20+ years of government trust and security clearance are assets money can't buy

buyReasons.title: Growth Accelerating, Not Decelerating
buyReasons.rationale: From FY2025 +56% to FY2026 +65% guidance. Revenue growth is actually speeding up, a rare quality

buyReasons.title: Commercial Segment Exploding
buyReasons.rationale: US commercial revenue surpassed $500M. AIP Bootcamps rapidly acquiring enterprise clients, healthily reducing government dependency

buyReasons.title: Fortress Balance Sheet
buyReasons.rationale: Cash $7.18B, debt $229M, FCF margin 47%. Strong earnings, minimal debt, overflowing cash

buyReasons.title: Wall Street Target Upgrades Trending Up
buyReasons.rationale: Consensus $196 (+30% upside). Morgan Stanley $205, UBS $200, Wedbush $230. Major brokerages raising targets

overallOpinion: Maven AI is now the official US military system, combined with Iran combat deployment. Motley Fool notes 'it looks expensive but investors keep buying for a reason'
Morgan Stanley $205, UBS $200, Wedbush $230 show growing bullishness, but one analyst warns of 55% downside to $70. Valuation opinions are extremely polarized
FY2026 +65% growth guidance, $11.2B backlog, 47% FCF margin. Fundamentals are best-in-class, but P/E 239x means one earnings miss could trigger a sharp drop
Below both 50-day ($152) and 200-day ($163) but RSI at 51 neutral. Strong fundamental momentum makes pullbacks an accumulation opportunity

investmentVerdict.summary: Maven military adoption, combat validation, +65% growth acceleration, and commercial expansion make fundamentals the strongest ever. P/E 239x and the $70 bear warning are concerns, but for this irreplaceable defense AI monopoly, buying on dips with position sizing discipline is recommended.

sources.description: Analysis of why investors keep buying despite absurd valuation

sources.description: Morgan Stanley $205 target, Hold with increased optimism

sources.description: Exclusive: Pentagon Maven AI adoption report

sources.description: Silicon Valley defense AI investments, Palantir combat deployment

sources.name: Motley Fool
sources.description: $11.2B revenue backlog, $10B Army contract, 61% growth outlook

sources.name: Motley Fool
sources.description: PLTR's one problem (valuation) and why to buy anyway

x.hook: P/E 239x: one says 'cheap,' another says '55% crash'
x.text:
$PLTR (Mar 23) Maven adopted by US military. Morgan Stanley $205 bullish. But one analyst says $70, warning of 55% drop. $150.68. Opinions are wildly divided.

1/4
thread:
+65% growth guidance, $11.2B backlog, 47% FCF margin. By fundamentals alone, this is the strongest AI stock.

2/4

Motley Fool: 'Looks expensive but there's no alternative.' P/E 239x is the concern, but earnings growth can normalize it fast.

3/4

Below 50/200-day, RSI 51 neutral. Target $196 (+30%). Buy on dips, exit quickly on earnings miss.

4/4

Below 50/200-day. Target $196 (+30%). If you believe in defense AI monopoly scarcity value, buy on dips.

witty.hook: Target range $70 to $230. Wall Street can't agree.
witty.text:
$PLTR (Mar 23) One analyst says $70 (-55%). Another says $230 (+53%). Are they looking at the same stock? $150.68.

1/4
thread:
Combat-proven. Military-adopted. +65% growth. The company is flawless. The price tag is the only flaw.

2/4

P/E 239x. Everyone knows it's expensive. Everyone also knows there's no alternative. That stalemate IS the stock price.

3/4

RSI 51 neutral. Target $196 (+30%). Whether it's cheap or expensive, we'll know in a year.

4/4

empathy.hook: PLTR holders: don't let the '55% crash' headline shake you
empathy.text:
$PLTR (Mar 23) '55% downside possible' headline dropped. If you're holding, your heart probably skipped a beat. $150.68.

1/4
thread:
But that's 1 of 21 analysts. The rest target $196-$230. Maven adopted, +65% growth. The company is executing.

2/4

P/E 239x is a real concern. One earnings miss and it drops hard. That risk has to be acknowledged.

3/4

Target $196 (+30%). If you're in it for the long term, buy on dips. Position sizing is everything.

4/4

empathy.hook: Don't let the '55% crash' headline shake you
empathy.text:
$PLTR (Mar 23) $150.68

'55% downside' made your heart skip. But that's 1 out of 21 analysts. Maven adopted, +65% growth, 47% FCF margin. The company is doing great.

P/E 239x is real risk. Acknowledge it. But fundamentals speak for themselves.

Target $196 (+30%). Buy on dips for the long term. Position sizing is key.
